title: A model of social stress in dominant mice

screening.reason: Reports behavioral outcomes in mice, but lacks automated monitoring details.
matched_inclusion_criteria: Reports behavioral, social, preference, dominance, activity, or interaction outcomes

overall_concept_summary: The study investigates the behavior of dominant mice under stress conditions without detailed description of an automated home-cage monitoring system.
relevance_to_review: Weakly relevant as it reports social behavior outcomes but does not use automated home-cage monitoring.
key_takeaways: The study focuses on the behavior of dominant mice under stress conditions, providing insights into social stress models in mice.
objective: To model social stress in dominant mice
limitations: No automated monitoring or behavioral outcome details provided.
missing_information: Automated home-cage monitoring methods and outcomes
